text.title: Client Conventions Intake Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules every client conventions intake report must satisfy.

rule.statement: The intake MUST capture 8 dimensions: branching, commit-style, code-review (SLA + reviewers), naming, CI gates, security policy, documentation conventions, communication norms. Missing dimension = report incomplete.
rule.rationale: PRs are rejected for any of these 8; missing one is enough to lose a sprint.

rule.statement: Every answer cites its source: either a repo file (path + line) or a stated answer from a named contact + date. Unsourced answers = report unfit for downstream automation.
rule.rationale: Without source citation, the report degrades to opinion; future PR conflicts have no anchor.

rule.statement: The client's named contact signs off on the final report (email + ISO date). Sign-off transforms the report from "what I thought you meant" to "what we agreed".
rule.rationale: Sign-off is what prevents downstream "we never said that" disputes.

rule.statement: Each captured convention must be cross-checked against repo observations (last 30 days of merged PRs). Mismatches between stated and observed are flagged in a `mismatches` field; the contact must resolve each one before sign-off.
rule.rationale: "We squash-merge" stated + last 10 PRs are merge-commits is a high-value finding; surfacing it forces the conversation.

rule.statement: The report is committed to the repo (or shared dropbox if no commit access) with semver version + last_reviewed date. Quarterly re-intake refreshes the record; >90 days unreviewed = stale.
rule.rationale: Conventions drift; static records become liabilities. Forced cadence keeps the artefact alive.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (repo access, contact person, engagement scope), execute: interview → capture → cross-check repo → sign-off → commit. Do not skip cross-check; it is where most surprises surface.
rule.rationale: Intakes without observed-vs-stated step ship blind to "what the team actually does".

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) single-PR fix engagement, (b) up-to-date CONVENTIONS.md exists < 90 days, (c) client refuses to engage — escalate the engagement, (d) existing intake record < 90 days old.
rule.rationale: Intake overhead requires engagement length + cooperation.
